page: Amazon Web Services c8id.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing a resource ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor.

The hardware configuration yields strong multi-core performance and memory throughput. In benchmark testing, the instance achieved top-tier results in multi-core memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached read operations, alongside top-tier multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core scores. Its CPU capabilities are further demonstrated by top-tier performance in Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations. Single-core performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, remains in the strong to average categories, indicating that workloads benefit most from parallelized execution.

For specialized workloads, the processor supports Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which contribute to top-tier prompt processing speeds in large language model inference across various model sizes. However, text generation speeds for these models scale down to average levels, highlighting a performance tradeoff between input processing and sequential token generation. The local NVMe SSD storage supports high-throughput operations, as evidenced by top-tier results in Passmark database operations and Redis SET performance.

The c8id.24xlarge offers high resource density for compute-heavy and storage-intensive tasks. The combination of dedicated high-frequency Intel Xeon cores, fast DDR5 memory, and substantial local NVMe SSD storage makes this instance highly efficient for parallel processing without requiring external storage attachments. It is well-suited for database hosting, software compilation, ray tracing, high-throughput web serving, and matrix-intensive mathematical workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.24xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory, 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor. The CPU includes AVX-512 and AMX extensions, driving top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, database operations, and LLM prompt processing. While single-core execution and sequential LLM text generation perform at average to strong levels, the instance excels in highly parallelized workloads. The high-density local storage and memory bandwidth make it highly efficient for data-intensive tasks. The c8id.24xlarge is optimized for database hosting, software compilation, ray tracing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: A high-frequency compute-optimized platform featuring dense local NVMe storage and advanced matrix extensions for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 virtual CPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz
192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core performance, database operations, and memory bandwidth
Hardware-accelerated AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for matrix operations
Optimized for database hosting, software compilation, and parallel computing
